items.title: Sinner critical of Madrid Open match scheduling
items.summary: World No. 1 Jannik Sinner is calling for better scheduling of matches at the Madrid Open, saying the night session "messes up the whole day" for players.
items.whyItMatters: It adds variety to the sports mix without turning the page into a full sports wire.

items.title: Chelsea reach FA Cup final; Serie A facing another ref scandal; Arsenal win ugly; more
items.summary: Chelsea advanced to the FA Cup final days after changing managers (again). Plus: Is Serie A heading for another referee scandal, and can Arsenal hang on in the English title race?
items.whyItMatters: Football creates regular global sports momentum and quick-return storylines.

items.title: Do Phillies or Red Sox have a better chance of turning it around? Weighing the odds after managerial firings
items.summary: Both struggling bottom-dwellers have fired their managers in recent days
items.whyItMatters: It adds variety to the sports mix without turning the page into a full sports wire.
